description_long: This is a short section of a classic white picket fence, commonly found in suburban and urban residential areas. The fence consists of five vertical pickets, each with a pointed top, evenly spaced and attached to two horizontal rails. The pickets and rails are made of wood, painted in a uniform white color, giving the fence a clean and traditional appearance. The surface shows subtle wood grain texture, indicating a natural material beneath the paint. The overall structure is lightweight and not very tall, designed more for decorative or boundary-marking purposes than for security. There are no visible decorative embellishments or gate mechanisms present.
description: A white wooden picket fence section with five pointed pickets and two horizontal rails, featuring a clean, traditional look.
description_view_0: Front view showing five evenly spaced, pointed pickets attached to two horizontal rails, all painted white.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence, with the pickets and rails aligned in a flat plane.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the same five pointed pickets and two rails.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim edge of the fence structure.
